Attached are a short trace bundle and a couple of sample 500 logs. Can you confirm if anything changed on your side (routing, peering, or proxy policies)? This needs executive visibility — our VP of Engineering wants an update in 30 minutes.

—Susan

Subject: Production: on‑prem cluster p95 spike + 503s — immediate help needed

Hi Camila / Marcus,

We started seeing a sudden jump in p95 latency and intermittent 503s on our Cedarline on‑prem cluster around 08:50 PT. Key symptoms: sustained CPU steal on the inference nodes, NVidia GPU utilization is high but token throughput dropped, and the worker processes started returning 503s for short windows (2–3 minutes) every ~10 minutes.

Attached is a short trace and sample nginx/worker logs (trace-20280311.log). We haven't changed traffic patterns or model versions in the last 48h. This is impacting multiple product teams — p95 moved from ~420ms to ~1.8s and error rate climbed to 2.1% on producti...

Subject: Greenline Health: elevated P99 latency & increased 5xx after model flip

Team —

Greenline's telemetry spiked right after we flipped to the quantized v2 candidate in prod at 21:30 PT. Highlights:
- P99 latency increased from ~210ms to ~620ms for text-generation route.
- 5xx rate rose from 0.2% to 1.8% (mostly OOM-ish kernel failures and a few 502s).
- Throughput dropped on their dedicated pool; queue depth went up.

I attached a short CSV with p95/p99 buckets for the 30m before/after. Can infra/runtime confirm whether this looks like a kernel selection regression or a capacity mis-balance (too many hot shards on same node)?

1) Karthik — can we rollback the...

Hi Ava,

Attached is TileVan's redline of the MSA (TileVan_Draft_MSA_redline.docx). Our legal team is taking a firm position on two points that are non-negotiable for our board:

1) Governing law: TileVan requires New York law to govern the agreement.
2) No mandatory arbitration as to claims for injunctive relief — our counsel needs an express carve so we can seek emergency relief in state or federal court in New York.

We are happy to consider arbitration for ordinary disputes, but we need a carveout so that we can preserve the ability to obtain temporary and injunctive relief quickly for IP and data protection issues.
